overview: Bant Spirits is an aggressive tempo deck in Pioneer that leverages Collected Company to flood the board with flying creatures and lords. It utilizes flash creatures for disruption and card advantage while aiming to close games quickly between turns four and five. The deck adapts its posture between an aggressive attack plan and a disruptive, value-oriented strategy depending on the matchup.
win_conditions: Finish the game between turns 4 and 5 using aggressive flying creatures and lords
Flood the battlefield with Collected Company to overwhelm opponents
Leverage flash creatures like Rattlechains and Mausoleum Wanderer for disruption and race control

keep_criteria: Hands with Portable Hole or Skyclave Apparition
Aggressive hands with lords to play with Collected Company
Hands with Mausoleum Wanderer for early pressure
Keep hands with 2-3 lands (preferably fetch/shock-compatible), at least one 1-drop threat (Skyclave Apparition, Phantom Warrior), and a mix of cheap spells or removal.
Keep hands with Thoughtseize/Inquisition if you have a solid early curve.
mulligan_criteria: Hands with Damping Sphere and Rest in Peace but only 3 mana value creatures
Hands lacking interaction or early threats
Mulligan pure land hands, hands with only 2-drops or higher, or hands with multiple removal but no threats or mana acceleration.

examples.reason: The article states that hands with Damping Sphere and Rest in Peace but only 3 mana value creatures are a correct mulligan against Lotus Combo.

early_game: Play flying creatures as fast as possible alongside Supreme Phantom
Use Collected Company on your turn to attack and remove blockers
Prioritize Mausoleum Wanderer for early pressure and protection
Turn 1: Play a land, cast a 1-drop threat or cast a cheap spell (Spell Pierce, Remand, or Thoughtseize) to grow your board and disrupt.
Turn 2: Play a shock land/fetch, cast a second threat or spot removal, and continue casting spells to grow your creatures.
Prioritize establishing a board of evasive threats while holding up interaction for key opponent plays.
mid_game: Use Collected Company as an answer to opponent's spells for disruption
Leverage flash creatures like Rattlechains and Spell Queller to stay ahead in the race
Trade efficiently to build card advantage
Optimize mana for shock lands and instant-speed interaction.
Use the graveyard strategically by not giving opponents free targets or recursion material.
Manage your hand by holding removal for key threats rather than burning it on small creatures.
Commit threats when you have interaction to answer their removal or when they're low on life.
Hold back if they have sweepers or heavy removal in hand.
late_game: Avoid late game against control due to limited card draw
Use Shapers' Sanctuary and Extraction Specialist to return dead creatures
Win through sustained card advantage and 2-to-1 trades
Supreme Phantom and Mausoleum Wanderer are primary finishers.
Once they're large enough (3+ counters), they can close out the game in 1-2 turns.
Use evasion and spot removal to clear the path and force trades.
Go all-in when you have a board of 2+ growing threats, they have no sweepers, and you're within lethal range.
Also commit when you've established graveyard advantage or when they're tapped out for a non-interactive spell.

key_cards.name: Collected Company
key_cards.notes: Used on your turn for aggression or as an answer for disruption; looks for answers like Spell Queller or aggressive lords.

key_cards.name: Supreme Phantom
key_cards.notes: Increases power of other spirits; used to finish quickly in the aggressive game plan.

key_cards.name: Mausoleum Wanderer
key_cards.notes: Helps finish games by turns 4-5; protects from removal and discards; can counter key cards like Pore Over the Pages.

key_cards.name: Spell Queller
key_cards.notes: Counters spells; held to answer Supreme Verdict or The Wandering Emperor.

key_cards.name: Rattlechains
key_cards.notes: Flash creature used to keep ahead in plays and the race.

key_cards.name: Shapers' Sanctuary
key_cards.notes: Enhances card advantage against spot removals; start with it to reduce opponent's spot removal potential.

key_cards.name: Portable Hole
key_cards.notes: Favors interaction with permanents; useful against opponent's copies or to prevent stabilization.

sequencing_tips: Use Collected Company on your turn to increase attack and remove blockers
Use Collected Company as an answer to opponent's spells for disruption
Hold Spell Queller to answer Supreme Verdict or The Wandering Emperor
Start the match with Mausoleum Wanderer to protect from removals and discards
Block creatures enchanted with Curious Obsession to prevent opponent card advantage
Use Shacklegeist to remove blockers like Old-Growth Troll
Common mistake: Overextending into sweepers or holding up too much mana for interaction.
Always track opponent's mana and hand size.
Use fetch/shock cycle efficiently to avoid flooding.
Supreme Phantom's late-game power is immense, so don't be afraid to cast it even if it seems slow early.
Remember that casting any instant/sorcery grows your board, so use removal and counterspells aggressively to maximize your threat size.

matchups.advice: Prioritize quickest strategy early, optimize tempo post-side, remove Shacklegeist and Remorseful Cleric, keep Portable Hole and use Shapers' Sanctuary with Extraction Specialist.
Apply fast board pressure, prefer hands with Portable Hole or Skyclave Apparition, side in Damping Sphere, use Shacklegeist on blockers.
Enhance Rattlechains and Mausoleum Wanderer to protect Supreme Phantom, trade 2-to-1 for card advantage, start with Shapers' Sanctuary, reduce 3-drop creatures.
watch_for: Fable of the Mirror-Breaker // Reflection of Kiki-Jiki

matchups.advice: Go for speed, prioritize Mausoleum Wanderer early, counter key cards with Spell Queller, mulligan to speed over combo prevention, side in Damping Sphere and Rest in Peace.

Keep battlefield creatures bigger than opponent's, block Curious Obsession enchanted creatures, side in Lifelink creatures, use Extraction Specialist for combat damage trades, use Portable Hole early.

Use cheap threats that can be cast in response to counterspells, or hold up interaction to protect your key spells. Use Thoughtseize to remove their counterspells. Consider Chalice of the Void on 1 or 2 to disrupt their mana or key spells.
Apply maximum pressure early before they can cast their big spells. Use spot removal to kill their ramp creatures. Sideboard in additional removal and hate cards to slow their acceleration.

Stabilize with spot removal and counterspells. Don't overcommit to the board. Use Leyline of the Meek or Rest in Peace to disrupt their graveyard/strategy. Transition to a control-oriented midgame if needed.

Pressure relentlessly. Use Thoughtseize to remove their counterspells and sweepers. Cast threats in response to their removal. Use evasion to bypass board wipes. Sideboard in additional pressure and hate cards to break their lock.

Win the resource war. Use Thoughtseize/Inquisition to strip their key spells. Prioritize graveyard advantage. Use Leyline of the Meek/Punishment to disrupt their strategy. Win the interaction war by holding up removal for their key threats and out-resourcing them.

common_threats: Fable of the Mirror-Breaker // Reflection of Kiki-Jiki
Watch for sweepers (Wrath of God, Anger of the Gods), graveyard hate (Rest in Peace, Leyline), and counterspells.

overview_evidence.evidence_span: As it is a deck that only has 7 noncreature spells, the Bant version becomes the most aggressive of Spirits lists, as all their creatures can go into the battlefield with Collected Company

win_conditions_evidence.evidence_span: With this play, it is possible to finish the game between turns 4 and 5, specially if there is a Mausoleum Wanderer in play.
all their creatures can go into the battlefield with Collected Company

sequencing_tips_evidence.evidence_span: favor using Collected Company on your turn to increase its attack and remove possible blockers.
use Collected Company as an answer to your opponents' spells, looking for good answers at that moment.
holding out our Spell Queller to answer Supreme Verdict or The Wandering Emperor.

common_threats_evidence.evidence_span: holding out our Spell Queller to answer Supreme Verdict or The Wandering Emperor.
We must remove Old-Growth Trolls and ramp units to delay our opponents from casting Cavalier of Thorns or Elder Gargaroth
deal with our opponent's threats, such as Sheoldred, the Apocalypse and Fable of the Mirror-Breaker // Reflection of Kiki-Jiki
